Claude Cowork Dispatch and Remote Control — Control Your AI Agent From Your Phone

Ever run into this situation? You're in the middle of working on something with Claude Cowork or Claude Code, then suddenly you need to leave your computer — maybe a meeting, going out for coffee, or just wanting to lie on the couch. Your AI Agent just sits there waiting for you to come back.
Not anymore. Anthropic launched two features:
(for Claude Code) that let you control your desktop AI Agent right from your phone.
In short, your phone becomes a remote, and the Claude on your computer is the machine being controlled. Just move your fingers and the AI takes care of all kinds of tasks on your computer.
The difference between Dispatch and Remote Control
Before we start, let's clarify the difference between these two features. Both are "control your computer remotely from your phone", but they're for different products:
Use the phone app to remotely operate Claude Desktop's Cowork features
Use the phone app or browser to remotely operate the local Claude Code terminal
is for regular users: ask Claude remotely to organize files, search Email, generate reports, and other everyday tasks.
is for developers: continue Claude Code dev tasks remotely — write code, debug, run tests, etc.
Dispatch — remotely control Cowork from your phone

between your phone app and the Claude Desktop on your computer. Think of it as a chat room — no matter where you are, just open the Claude App on your phone and you can issue commands directly to the Claude on your computer.

Your computer is what's actually doing the work — the phone is just a message interface. So Claude can access files, Connectors, plugins, and all the other resources on your computer — even stuff you can't open on your phone at all.
: install the latest version of the Claude desktop app (macOS or Windows x64)
: install the latest version of the Claude App (
: Pro or Max subscriptions can use it.
: your computer must be awake and Claude Desktop must be open. If you close your laptop or it goes into sleep mode, Dispatch disconnects.
Install/update Claude Desktop to the latest version (
Install/update the Claude phone App to the latest version (
If you want Claude to operate your browser, also install the Claude browser extension (
If you don't do these, you'll find Dispatch is either invisible or unclickable.
Then open Claude Desktop, switch to the "Cowork" tab, and you'll find "Dispatch" on the left side.
Just follow the on-screen instructions, pick up your phone, and you can tell Claude on your phone what you want done on your computer.
What can it do? Simple example — I want to ask it to find a screenshot I just took.

That's just a basic example, of course. There's a lot more it can do — organize files, search Email, generate reports, open apps. Anything you'd usually ask Claude to do, you can now assign directly from your phone.

If this looks similar to OpenClaw to you — yes, it does. The Dispatch concept is actually really similar to OpenClaw — both let you remotely control an AI Agent on your computer. The difference is Dispatch is an official Anthropic feature, so it's more stable and secure.

I think the biggest upside of using Cowork is you don't have to worry about being flagged as doing something against TOS and getting your account suspended — after all, you're using their official feature.
Heads up though — Dispatch essentially establishes a "trust chain". Commands you send from your phone really do trigger
on your computer, including reading, moving, and even deleting local files. So it's not just "looking" — it really can "act".

So before enabling, confirm whether you trust every app and service in this chain, whether you understand which files and accounts Claude can access, and whether you know how to disconnect or revoke access — to avoid Claude accidentally deleting something important.

Dispatch has some current limitations. The task stops once the computer goes to sleep. Claude only responds passively (it won't notify you when a task is done — you have to open your phone to check). Currently it only supports one Dispatch conversation at a time (no parallel threads). The phone won't get any push notifications. And scheduled tasks need to be configured separately in Cowork — you can't manage scheduling from Dispatch.
Also, files Claude generates after completing tasks can be accessed directly from your phone, or back on your computer at the location Claude specified.
Remote Control — the developer's remote tool
If Dispatch is the remote for regular users, Remote Control is the remote for developers.
Remote Control lets you connect to a Claude Code Session running on your local machine via
or the Claude phone App. The core concept is:
Claude Code keeps running on your local machine
— your file system, MCP Servers, project settings are all still there. The phone or browser is just a "window".
This also means your data doesn't get moved to the cloud — all tasks complete on your computer.
A great thing about Remote Control is it supports
. If your laptop accidentally goes to sleep or your network drops temporarily, the Session auto-reconnects when the computer comes back online — no manual intervention needed.
: Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise can all use it. But for Team or Enterprise plans, the admin needs to first enable Remote Control at
: at minimum, you need to have run
once in the project directory and accepted the Workspace trust dialog
Remote Control has three start methods. Pick the one that fits your needs.
Method 1: enable from an existing Session (most common)
If you're already in a Claude Code conversation and suddenly want to continue from your phone, just type:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
works too. This method preserves your current conversation history and converts it to a Remote Control Session directly.
Method 2: start with the remote feature on
If you know upfront you'll want to operate from your phone, add the parameter when launching Claude Code:
This way you can keep typing commands in the terminal as normal, while also operating the same Session from a phone or browser. You can also give the Session a name:
If you want your computer to act purely as a remote server standing by, use:
In this mode the terminal goes into Server mode, dedicated to waiting for remote connections. You can press the spacebar to show the QR Code. Server mode also supports some extra parameters:
Sets the Session name, shown in the Session list at claude.ai/code
Sets the creation method for multiple Sessions.
makes each Session use an independent git worktree
Sets the max simultaneous Session count, default 32
Shows detailed connection and Session logs
Enables or disables sandbox mode, default off
The difference between Server mode and Method 2: in Server mode you
can't enter commands in the local terminal
— it's purely a server waiting for remote connections. In Method 2, both local and remote can operate at the same time.
Let me demo Method 1. First, go to your project directory and type:
language-txt shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

Remote control lets you access this CLI session via the web (claude.ai/code) or the Claude app, so you can pick up unfinished progress from any device.

Just run /remote-control again to disconnect remote access at any time.

1. Enable Remote Control for this session (Opens a secure connection to claude.ai)
2. Not now (you can type /remote-control to enable again anytime)

Pick "1. Enable Remote Control for this session — Opens a secure connection to claude.ai" — that activates Remote Control.
Then you get a URL — paste it into your phone's browser, and you can operate this project directly from your phone.
You can also use the QR Code scan method — just type
again to see the "Show QRCode" option.
Of course, if you don't like the URL approach, you can open the Claude phone APP and find Code in the sidebar — you'll see the Session you just created.
Whichever method you use, remember to turn off Remote when not using it — to avoid potential security issues.
Auto-enable Remote Control on every launch

Once this setting is on, every Claude Code interactive process you open auto-registers a Remote Session. If you have several Claude Codes open at once, each one has its own independent Session. If you want to manage multiple Sessions through a single process, you have to use Server mode with the
You might worry that remote control has security issues — let me briefly explain.
In short, Claude Code only establishes

— it doesn't open any inbound port on your computer. When you start Remote Control, it registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server uses a streaming connection to relay messages between the Web/phone end and your local Session. All traffic goes through the Anthropic API over
— same security level as your normal Claude Code usage. The connection uses
, each with independent scope and expiration time. So security isn't something to worry too much about.
Remote Control vs Claude Code on the Web
You might be curious — what's the difference between Remote Control and just using Claude Code on the Web at
? Both seem to use the same interface.
Mid-development local work, need to step away temporarily
Quickly start a task without local environment needed

In short, if you're in the middle of dev work and need to leave your computer, Remote Control is the right call. But if you just want to quickly fire off a new task, work on a repo you haven't cloned, or run multiple tasks in parallel, just using Claude Code on the Web is more convenient.
Remote Control has limits too. The terminal must stay open (closing the terminal or stopping the
process ends the Session). If your computer is awake but loses network for over about 10 minutes, the Session auto-times out (then you have to re-run
to create a new Session). And in non-Server mode, each Claude Code instance only supports one Remote Session (to manage multiple at once you need Server mode with
If you hit issues using Remote Control, here are common error messages and fixes:
"Remote Control is not yet enabled for your account"
This is usually environment variables messing things up. Check whether
are set — unset them and try again. Also if you've set
, note that Remote Control needs claude.ai authentication — it can't be used with third-party providers.
If none of that applies, try
"Remote Control is disabled by your organization's policy"
Three possible causes for this error. First — you might be logged in via API Key or Console account, but Remote Control needs claude.ai OAuth. So run
and pick the claude.ai option (also unset
if your environment has it set).
Second — Team or Enterprise admins haven't enabled it yet. Remote Control is off by default in these plans, so the admin needs to go to
to enable it. Note: the Remote Control toggle depends on the Claude Code on the Web toggle, so you have to enable Claude Code on the Web first.

Third — the admin toggle is grayed out, meaning your organization has incompatible data retention or compliance settings. This case can't be modified from the admin panel — you need to contact Anthropic support.
This means Claude Code can't get short-lived credentials from the Anthropic API to establish the connection. Add
Common causes include not being logged in (run

), firewall or proxy possibly blocking outbound HTTPS requests (Remote Control needs to access the Anthropic API on port 443), or Session creation failure (if you also see "Session creation failed — see debug log", that means the problem is at an earlier setup stage — first verify your subscription is active).
The core concept of both Dispatch and Remote Control is the same —
don't be tied to your computer anymore

If you're a developer, I'd really recommend trying Remote Control — especially when you suddenly need to leave your computer but don't want to break your dev flow, it's super convenient. Dispatch lets you assign tasks from your phone during a commute and come back to your seat to find the work done — also a really practical feature.
One more thing — if you don't want to use Cowork or Claude Code, you can consider a project I built:

Ever thought about controlling the AI Agent on your computer from your phone? Anthropic recently launched two big features — "Dispatch" and "Remote Control" — that let you remotely control Claude Cowork and Claude Code from your phone. Even when you're away from your computer, you can assign tasks, monitor progress, and come back to a finished result. This post walks through the differences between the two features, how to set them up, and real-world use cases.
